description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a prominent red gable roof made of metal panels. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and functional appearance. The front features two large open bays for animal entry and exit, as well as a smaller enclosed section with a window. The base of the structure is finished with a brick or stone foundation, providing stability and protection from moisture. There is a single rectangular window with a grid pattern on one side, and the overall design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for housing livestock. The roof overhangs slightly, offering additional shelter at the entrances.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red gable metal roof, open bays, and a brick foundation, designed for livestock shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large open bays and a smaller enclosed section with a window, highlighting the main entry points.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the gable end, a single window, and the transition from open bays to enclosed space.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a continuous wooden wall with no visible openings or windows, emphasizing the building's enclosure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain gable end and no windows, mirroring the other side but without the window feature.
attribute_door_style: large open bays with no visible doors, suitable for livestock entry
attribute_window_presence: one rectangular window on the side, grid style
